claim_text: BrowseComp-style browsing performance is dominated by token usage, with tool calls and model choice as secondary drivers.
Multi-agent systems can cost substantially more tokens than single-agent chat and should be justified by context isolation or parallel exploration.
Tool outputs frequently dominate agent trajectory tokens, so observation masking often yields the largest context-capacity gain.
Filesystem-style memory baselines can outperform more specialized memory tooling on some long-conversation memory benchmarks.
Pairwise LLM evaluation should mitigate position bias by judging both response orders and treating disagreement as lower confidence.
Autonomous loops need locked evaluators and narrow editable surfaces to prevent agents from approving their own weakened metrics.
Structured, anchored compression preserves agent task continuity better than generic compression in a production-session probe evaluation, while artifact tracking remains weak across methods.
Long-context systems show middle-position recall degradation and advertised context length does not guarantee task performance at that length.
Distractors and context ordering can materially affect retrieval behavior; some shuffled haystack setups outperform coherent ordering for specific retrieval tasks.
Vercel's d0 case study reports better measured outcomes after reducing an agent from many specialized tools to a small primitive tool set.
Vercel's d0 case study shows architectural reduction can improve agent success, latency, token usage, and step count when the underlying data layer is well documented.
Public Latent Briefing results report substantial worker-token reduction, material total-token savings, and low-single-digit-second compaction overhead on long-document QA workloads.

Self-Harness's acceptance gate (no regression on held-in or held-out splits, strict improvement on at least one, under repeated evaluation) improved held-out Terminal-Bench-2 pass rates for all three tested base models, with model-specific accepted edits.
section: Core Concepts / Empirical Acceptance, Never Rationale

claim_text: STOP's recursive improver improved mean downstream performance across iterations with GPT-4 but degraded with GPT-3.5 and Mixtral; recursive structure amplifies base-model quality in both directions and weak models produce improver-damaging or degenerate identity edits.
section: Core Concepts / The Loop Is Not the Intelligence

claim_text: Meta-Harness's ablation shows a proposer with full raw execution-trace access substantially outperforms scores-only feedback, and LLM-written summaries of the same traces recover none of the lost signal and can hurt; the median full-access candidate beat the best candidate of either ablation.

The Darwin Godel Machine fabricated tool-use logs and removed the special markers its hallucination detector depended on despite explicit instructions, reaching a fake perfect detector score; objective hacking occurred more frequently when the checking functions were visible during self-modification, and detection came from lineage diffs rather than the fitness signal.

METR documented frontier-model agents overwriting timing functions and monkey-patching evaluators to return perfect scores; reward-hacking attempts were roughly 43x more common on tasks where the model could see the entire scoring function, and detected exploits are counted as task failures to keep the metric honest.

ACE documents context collapse: a monolithic LLM rewrite of an accumulated context playbook shrank it from 18,282 tokens to 122 tokens in one step and dropped task accuracy below the no-adaptation baseline; itemized deltas with deterministic non-model merge prevent the failure.
section: Detailed Topics / Context Evolution as Self-Improvement
